content:
Dear Keepers,

Thank you so much for your incredible enthusiasm and support for our community volunteer translation project! 
We're thrilled to officially open volunteer applications and invite you to join us.

✦ You can find all the recruitment details and sign-up form here: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeySqAR_beH4ldIxTz7egq2SaJTplTnJHdrPGBNz2I8yY68vQ/viewform

Keepers who participate will receive a special thank you in the in-game credits page. Plus, we'll be providing additional rewards for our volunteers (details subject to final confirmation).

✦ Important Notes:
1. Please understand that organizing a volunteer team, especially a large one, requires significant time and manpower on our end. Because of this, we won't be able to accept every applicant right away, but we appreciate your patience as we sort through submissions. You'll hear from us as soon as we can! 
2. Project reward content may be adjusted based on actual circumstances, with official announcements being final.

We're incredibly grateful to collaborate with our dedicated community and make Morimens accessible to even more players!

embeds.title: Help Localize Morimens! Volunteer Interest Form
embeds.description:
Dear Keeper, thank you for your interest in joining this volunteer project!
This short form helps us organize the volunteer translation effort. Your passion means the world to us.
~Ace

thread_title: Call for Community Volunteer Translators: Applications Open!

content:
<:dishes:1356083313063759922> Thank you for your support, Keepers! 💙

The application form will stay open indefinitely, so please feel free to apply whenever you're ready.
PS: We're especially looking for two key roles right now!

1️⃣ Moderators
Managed community projects or Discord servers before?
Your organizational skills would be invaluable! (Especially if you speak both English and another language available in game!)

2️⃣ Testers
Have time to playtest in-game translations?
Love polishing text and catching errors?
We need your sharp eyes!

Check the 'Roles' section in the form for details.
Again, we’re truly grateful for your help!
thread_title: Call for Community Volunteer Translators: Applications Open!

So if github/gitlab becomes the preferred platform for sharing things, I can get the volunteers up to speed with it if needed.

*However I use git as a command line tool so for windows users I can only help them with "git bash" & general github/gitlab use and not the graphical interface that exists*

Especially since stigma and unfamiliarity with how games work could get one accused of illegitimacy. thread_title: Call for Community Volunteer Translators: Applications Open!

content: Also, the thank you message will not contain my legal name, but the recognizable alias I put down. Thus complicating proof of identity further.
thread_title: Call for Community Volunteer Translators: Applications Open!

content: Thus, it is best if there is out of game correspondence and acknowledgement from company side. Which,  as far as my sleep deprived brain can tell, circumvents the above issues.

content: Finally, if that wasn’t reason enough, if my account becomes hacked, terminated, or inaccessible in any other way, I’d still have proof of work for my resume.

thread_title: Call for Community Volunteer Translators: Applications Open!

content:
Hey Keeper,

Absolutely. If you eventually join us, I'd be more than happy to provide a professional reference, letter of recommendation, or any other support you need to highlight your work with us on your resume. I'm really glad you're looking to showcase this experience!

Before I joined the Morimens team, I also worked on the EN localization for Wuthering Waves and Love & Deepspace. (Despite our small budget, I just really love Morimens. It's the best decision I've ever made.) 
So if becoming a game translator is something you're considering for your future, please know I'm very willing to share my experiences and help however I can!

Honestly, our team genuinely wishes we could hire more professional translators, and I totally get that asking for volunteers isn't ideal. It's something I hesitated with myself because we can't offer proper compensation yet. 
Our budget just doesn't allow for it right now, but we truly hope to change that in the future. Until then, the support from Keepers like you is incredibly valuable, and I'm genuinely so grateful for all your help.
thread_title: Call for Community Volunteer Translators: Applications Open!

content: Thank you truly, Ace. I’d be more than happy to learn all that I can from you. <:pray:1356053116260454534>
thread_title: Call for Community Volunteer Translators: Applications Open!

content: What's the difference between language tester and proofreader? Normally I would assume language tester works more on sentiment of message being effectively translated but your examples are "bugs and cutoffs" which seems more just like further proofreading
thread_title: Call for Community Volunteer Translators: Applications Open!

content: The way I read it is proofreading can be done on blocks of text, the cutoffs & bugs require seeing the text in-game (or hopefully in an interface that replicates the way it displays it in-game).
thread_title: Call for Community Volunteer Translators: Applications Open!

content: so it's more of a "*this text breaks in the UI*" vs "*you misspelled X's name there*"
